creator: freebsd7-pr@i-cnct.com
text:
Date: Wed, 5 Mar 2008 21:58:06 -0700 (MST)
 X-send-pr-version: 3.113
 X-GNATS-Notify:
 
 > Submitter-Id:  current-users
 > Originator:    Esoga Simmons <freebsd7-pr@i-cnct.com>
 > Organization:  None
 > Confidential:  no
 > Synopsis:      [WORKAROUND] /boot/loader Can't work out which disk to 
 boot
 > Severity:      serious
 > Priority:      medium
 > Category:      kern
 > Class:         sw-bug
 > Release:       FreeBSD 7.0-STABLE i386
 > Environment:
 System: FreeBSD HOSTNAME 7.0-STABLE FreeBSD 7.0-STABLE #0: Wed Mar 5 18:47:36 
 MST 2008 freebsd7-pr@i-cnct.com:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/MEG i386
 > Description:
    read /usr/src/updating
    read all current pr reports
    previous OS FreeBSD 6.3
    make.conf has no build options (although have also built with NO_PROFILE=true 
 and CFLAGS= -0 -pipe but no difference in results)
 
    building from src
 
 #  1.  `cd /usr/src'       (or to the directory containing your source tree).
 #  2.  `make buildworld'
 #  3.  `make buildkernel KERNCONF=YOUR_KERNEL_HERE'     (default is GENERIC).
 #  4.  `make installkernel KERNCONF=YOUR_KERNEL_HERE'   (default is GENERIC).
 #       [steps 3. & 4. can be combined by using the "kernel" target]
 #  5.  `reboot'        (in single user mode: boot -s from the loader prompt).
 #  6.  `mergemaster -p'
 #  7.  `make installworld'
 #  8.  `make delete-old'
 #  9.  `mergemaster'
 # 10.  `reboot'
 
 After final mergemaster and reboot, /boot/loader gives error:
 
 Guessed BIOS device 0x%x not found by probes, defaulting to disk0:
 Can't work out which disk we are booting from.
 
 [WORKAROUND]
 
 If I interrupt the boot process before this and boot from:
 
 boot: /boot/loader.old
 
 or use the fix-it disk and copy loader to loader.sav, loader.old to loader
 
 # fsck_ffs /dev/da0s
 # mkdir /mnt/root
 # mount -rw /dev/da0s1a /mnt/root
 # mv /mnt/root/boot/loader /mnt/root/boot/loader.sav
 # cp /mnt/root/boot/loader.old /mnt/root/boot/loader
 
 I can then boot
 
 # dmesg

 > How-To-Repeat:
 
 Happens on new install of FreeBSD6.3 and upgrade to FreeBSD7 using src build.
 
 Have other system that is similar, but build works.  Have seen some other 
 similar problem reports
 
 http://groups.google.com/group/lucky.freebsd.questions/browse_thread/thread/f18554c471e8d29e/bbb6c94e7b6e655d?lnk=raot
 
 
 > Fix:
 
 [WORKAROUND]
 
 Break out of the boot process before the failure and:
 
 boot: /boot/loader.old
 
 Then
 # mv /boot/loader /boot/loader.sav
 # cp /boot/loader.old /boot/loader
